meanings.definition: The money, resources, ability, or means needed to do something.
examples: They lacked the financial wherewithal to continue the project.
She had the wherewithal to build her own business.
Many talented students do not have the wherewithal for higher education.
The team finally gained the technical wherewithal to complete the task.

meanings.definition: Coming to rest, becoming calm, or moving into a stable position.
examples: Dust was slowly settling on the old furniture.
The baby finally settled after hours of crying.
Snow had settled across the rooftops overnight.
The crowd was settling down before the performance began.

meanings.definition: Resolving or deciding something such as an argument, payment, or problem.
examples: They were settling the dispute peacefully.
She settled the bill before leaving the restaurant.
The lawyers are settling the case outside court.
He finally settled his differences with his brother.

meanings.definition: Beginning to live permanently in a place.
examples: Their family settled in the village generations ago.
Travelers were settling near the river for water and farmland.
She plans on settling abroad after graduation.
The settlers had settled the region long before the city was built.

meanings.definition: Traveled on or controlled something such as a horse, bicycle, or vehicle.
examples: He had ridden horses since childhood.
They were riding bicycles through the countryside.
She rode the train to work every morning.
The travelers had ridden across the desert for days.

meanings.definition: Filled, troubled, or burdened with something unpleasant.
examples: The city was crime-ridden for many years.
He looked guilt-ridden after the argument.
The village remained poverty-ridden despite government promises.
Stress-ridden workers struggled to maintain balance in life.

meanings.definition: Familiar with someone, something, or having knowledge about it.
examples: She is well acquainted with classical literature.
The guide acquainted visitors with the history of the temple.
I became acquainted with him during college.
Workers must be acquainted with safety rules before entering the factory.

meanings.definition: Introduced someone to another person, place, idea, or piece of information.
examples: The teacher acquainted students with the new syllabus.
Travel acquainted him with different cultures.
She was acquainting herself with the city before starting work.
The documentary acquainted viewers with ancient traditions.

meanings.definition: Dull, gloomy, depressing, or lacking excitement and hope.
examples: Rain fell over the dreary town all afternoon.
He spent years in a drear office doing repetitive work.
The novel describes a bleak and dreary winter landscape.
Her voice sounded tired and drearily hopeless.

meanings.definition: Respect, admiration, or high regard for someone or something.
examples: The professor was held in great esteem by students.
Years of honest work earned him public esteem.
She lost esteem for the dishonest politician.
Self-esteem plays an important role in confidence.

meanings.definition: To respect, admire, or value someone highly.
examples: People esteem her for her kindness and wisdom.
He was highly esteemed in scientific circles.
The community esteems teachers who work sincerely.
She esteemed honesty above wealth and status.

meanings.definition: Damaged the appearance of someone or something, often permanently.
examples: The fire disfigured several buildings in the town.
He was disfigured by injuries from the accident.
Pollution is disfiguring the natural landscape.
The statue had been badly disfigured over time.
The disfigured wall showed signs of years of neglect.
She cared for animals with disfigured limbs.
The once beautiful garden looked disfigured after the storm.
His face remained slightly disfigured after surgery.

meanings.definition: Damaged, ruined, or made something less enjoyable or useful.
examples: Heavy rain spoilt the picnic plans.
The milk had spoiled because it was left outside.
Careless handling spoiled the painting.
Noise from the street was spoiling the peaceful atmosphere.

meanings.definition: Given too much attention or luxury, resulting in selfish or bad behavior.
examples: The spoilt child refused to share his toys.
People considered him spoiled because he always got what he wanted.
Her spoilt attitude annoyed the other students.
The wealthy family raised several spoiled pets.
